title: tragia involucrata var. genuina
intro: Tragia involucrata var. genuina, also known as the Wild Balsam Apple, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Tragia involucrata var. genuina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the leaves and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
